items.headline: Colorado Avalanche Carolina Hurricanes make statement as top Stanley Cup contenders
items.description: Legendary hockey reporter Stan Fischler writes a weekly scrapbook for NHL.com. Also known as "The Hockey Maven," Fischler delivers his wit and insight to readers each Wednesday. This week is about the Colorado Avalanche and Carolina Hurricanes after they swept the Los Angeles Kings and Ottawa Senators, respectively, to advance

items.headline: Bruins David Pastrnak breakaway overtime goal Game 5
items.description:
David Pastrnak had the sauce on Tuesday.
The Boston Bruins forward scored an impressive breakaway goal in overtime during Game 5 of the Eastern Conference First Round against the Buffalo Sabres at KeyBank Center.
Around nine minutes into overtime, Bruins defenseman Hampus Lindholm picked up the turned over puck at

items.headline: 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs first round schedule television results
items.description: The 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs are underway. Here is the complete schedule for the first round of the playoffs, including TV information and results:

items.headline: Minnesota Wild Dallas Stars Game 5 recap April 28, 2026
items.description: DALLAS -- Kirill Kaprizov had a goal and two assists, and the Minnesota Wild defeated the Dallas Stars 4-2 in Game 5 of the Western Conference First Round at American Airlines Center on Tuesday.​

items.headline: Start times for Stanley Cup Playoff games on May 1 announced
items.description:
NEW YORK – The National Hockey League today announced the following updates to the 2026 Stanley Cup Playoffs First Round schedule:
Friday, May 1
- The start time for Game 6 of the First Round series between the Tampa Bay Lightning and Montreal Canadiens has been set for 7 p.m.

items.headline: Boston Bruins Buffalo Sabres Game 5 recap April 28, 2026
items.description: BUFFALO -- David Pastrnak scored at 9:14 of overtime for the Boston Bruins, who stayed alive in the Eastern Conference First Round with a 2-1 win against the Buffalo Sabres in Game 5 at KeyBank Center on Tuesday.

items.headline: Jason Robertson, Stars honor veteran with trip to Game 5
items.description:
DALLAS -- Jason Robertson hosts a family of an active-duty service member at every Dallas Stars home game throughout the regular season. The program is called "JR's Heroes" and it's been ongoing since the start of the 2021-22 season.
"I don't think I've ever done it in the playoffs, honestly,"

items.headline: Coaches Room: Pittsburgh Penguins ride momentum into Game 6 against Flyers
items.description:
The Coaches Room is a regular feature throughout the 2025-26 season by former NHL coaches and assistants who turn their critical gaze to the game and explain it through the lens of a teacher.
In this edition, Dan Bylsma, former coach of the Pittsburgh Penguins, Buffalo Sabres and Seattle Kraken

items.headline: Buffalo Sabres fans sing O Canada before Game 5
items.description:
No audio, no problem in Buffalo.
Fans at KeyBank Center stepped up during the singing of “O Canada” before Game 5 of the Eastern Conference First Round on Tuesday after anthem singer Cami Clune’s microphone cut out.
Once the crowd realized what was going on, fans didn’t miss a beat

items.headline: Mats Zuccarello Yakov Trenin injury status for Minnesota for Game 5
items.description:
DALLAS -- Mats Zuccarello and Yakov Trenin returned for the Minnesota Wild in Game 5 of the Western Conference First Round against the Dallas Stars at American Airlines Center on Tuesday.
Zuccarello had missed the past three games and Trenin the past two, each with an upper-body injury.

items.headline: Tampa Bay Lightning Victor Hedman playing status update April 28 2026
items.description:
BRANDON, Fla. -- Victor Hedman couldn’t stop smiling Tuesday.
Neither could many of his Tampa Bay Lightning teammates.
The upbeat vibe surrounding the Lightning captain and his team had to do with the fact the defenseman was able to participate in a full practice, including the majority of drills at

items.headline: Juraj Slafkovsky to play for Montreal Canadiens in Game 5
items.description:
BROSSARD, Quebec -- Juraj Slafkovsky did not practice with the Montreal Canadiens on Tuesday but will be in the lineup for Game 5 of the Eastern Conference First Round at the Tampa Bay Lightning on Wednesday (7 p.m. ET; TheSpot, ESPN2, SN, TVAS, CBC).

items.description:

items.headline: Pittsburgh Penguins core has belief team can complete comeback vs Philadelphia Flyers
items.description:
CRANBERRY, Pa. -- Sidney Crosby, Evgeni Malkin and Kris Letang have nearly seen it all.
In their 20 seasons together with the Pittsburgh Penguins, they’ve won the Stanley Cup three times (2009, 2016, 2017). Crosby, in 2016 and 2017, and Malkin, in 2009, have won the Conn Smythe Trophy as
